description_long: This house features a classic gable roof with a steep pitch, covered in tan shingles and supported by visible wooden rafters and beams. The exterior walls are constructed from red brick, giving the structure a traditional and sturdy appearance. The roof structure is partially exposed, revealing a network of wooden trusses and cross-bracing, as well as some internal framing painted in green and pink. There are no visible windows or doors on the brick facades, and the house appears to be a single-story structure with a spacious attic or loft area. The overall design is simple and functional, with a focus on structural integrity and traditional materials. The absence of decorative elements or complex architectural features suggests a utilitarian approach to residential construction.
description: A red brick house with a steep gable roof, exposed wooden rafters, and a simple, traditional design. The structure emphasizes sturdy construction and lacks visible windows or decorative features.
description_view_0: The house is seen from the gable end, showing a solid red brick wall and the full height of the steeply pitched tan shingle roof.
description_view_1: This view reveals the roof structure with exposed wooden rafters and internal trusses, as well as the brick wall below.
description_view_2: The opposite gable end is visible, showing the interior roof framing and green-painted beams, with the brick wall at the base.
description_view_3: Similar to view 1, this side view highlights the exposed roof rafters, internal cross-bracing, and the brick wall beneath.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway not visible; likely a simple door on one of the brick facades, with no porch or decorative elements.
